title: Greek Lamb Kebabs
description: A traditional Greek dish featuring marinated lamb skewers, perfect for a summer BBQ. The combination of flavors and textures creates a delicious and exotic meal.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Make sure to trim any excess fat

ingredients.name: Greek yogurt
ingredients.notes: Use plain, full-fat yogurt for best results

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Extra virgin olive oil adds a rich flavor

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lemon juice is essential

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Use 2-3 cloves for a stronger flavor

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for a flaky texture

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper is best

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Sweet or smoked paprika adds a rich flavor

ingredients.name: Zest of 1 lemon
ingredients.notes: Use a vegetable peeler to get the zest

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Brush the grill with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare.
Transfer the kebabs to a plate and let them rest for 5 minutes.
Serve immediately, garnished with lemon wedges and a sprinkle of paprika.
